level_signals: Minimum 1 year of experience in growth, product management, product marketing or adjacent field

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Minimum 1 year of experience in growth, product management, product marketing or adjacent field
Demonstrated ability to dissect complex problems, identify solutions, and drive these solutions to completion with a high degree of autonomy
Proficiency with data analytics with an ability to draw key insights from both quantitative and qualitative data
High level of resourcefulness and a track record of being a fast learner
Communication skills to influence others at all levels and across all functions within the organization to rally behind shared business goals
Demonstrated ability to leverage AI to accelerate and improve your work – we'll ask about what you've built

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience owning a pipeline, revenue, or metric goal
Experience with no-code / low-code tools (eg Zapier, Webflow, Retool)

scope.surface: Ramp website — conversion rate optimization (CRO), landing pages, top-of-funnel demand generation
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / fintech — finance automation platform for businesses
primary_metrics_inferred: Net new leads generated via website
core_responsibilities: Drive net new leads for Ramp, with an emphasis on Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) on our website and Top-of-Funnel demand generation campaigns.
Own the feedback loop of testing new copy, imagery, layouts, and engagement mechanisms on our website, analyzing the results, and sharing learnings with our broader Growth & Marketing teams.
Leverage web analytics tools to monitor website performance and uncover trends and opportunities.

Find and build AI-powered workflows like finding experimentation opportunities, reporting on metrics to the broader growth team, and any other ways to make the Web/growth teams operate more effectively and efficiently.
Regularly report on key performance indicators (KPIs) such as conversion rates, user engagement, user traffic patterns, and sales lead generation.
Work across Sales, Marketing, Engineering, Product, Risk, and Operations to execute against Ramp's growth goals

green_flags: Clear ownership of a specific growth surface (website CRO and top-of-funnel)
Explicit encouragement of AI tool usage and builder mindset
Broad cross-functional exposure across Sales, Marketing, Engineering, Product, Risk, and Operations
Company discloses meaningful scale ($100B+ annualized spend, 50,000+ customers)
Comprehensive benefits package including relocation support to NYC or SF
Low minimum experience bar (1 year) with emphasis on demonstrated output over credentials
red_flags: Office time requirement is unclear — relocation support to NYC or SF is mentioned, suggesting in-office expectation, but not explicitly stated
Location type (hybrid vs remote) is ambiguous; benefits mention relocation to NYC or SF but JD does not explicitly state office policy

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 1-year minimum and scope of responsibilities)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities section, not explicitly listed as success metrics)
location.type (inferred as hybrid based on relocation support to NYC/SF; not explicitly stated)
location.cities_listed (inferred from relocation support mention; not explicitly listed as role locations)
ambiguities: Work location policy is not explicitly stated (hybrid vs onsite vs remote). Relocation support to NYC or SF is mentioned in benefits, suggesting an office-present expectation, but no office time percentage or hybrid label is given. Treated as hybrid.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; set to 'unclear'.
The JD mentions global benefits and Canada/UK-specific benefits, suggesting potential openness to non-US hiring, but the role does not explicitly state remote-global eligibility.
